summary.helper_layer_assessment: useful, with current browser duplication accepted until wrapper generation is implemented
summary.generated_artifact_browser_unification_u: optional after current Final Round public use; not urgent
summary.recommended_stopping_point: Stop at audit-only readiness. Preserve the current artifact-backed Final Round experience and defer deeper optimizer/captain extraction.

scores.evidence: Active-stage manifest and active QA runner pass 56/56; deployed public preview browser QA and exact Team Builder browser assertions passed after commit 66fcc22.
scores.remaining_risks: Live FIFA locks, deadlines, boosters, and final XIs are manual checks.
scores.recommended_action: Ship with caveats visible; do not rebuild models.

scores.evidence: Manifest search checks block old globals and active eliminated-player leakage; default matchday is finalRound.
scores.remaining_risks: Historical stage data remains loaded for context and can create noisy text-search hits.
scores.recommended_action: Keep validator explanations for historical hits.

scores.evidence: data/activeStageManifest_v1.json declares activeStage finalRound, required command checks, syntax checks, search checks, public wiring, and expected public behavior assertions.
scores.remaining_risks: Manifest maintenance is manual when adding new validators or wrappers.
scores.recommended_action: Continue treating the manifest as the release gate.

scores.evidence: Team Builder remains artifact-backed by default; golden, browser equivalence, eligibility, rules helper, constraint helper, and unification readiness validators pass.
scores.remaining_risks: User locks, substitutions, boosters, and played/unplayed official game state are not imported.

scores.evidence: Rules-derived limits moved into shared helpers; script.js line count reduced from 15351 to 15258.
scores.remaining_risks: Optimizer scoring and captain/vice ranking are still browser-side and should not be casually changed.
scores.recommended_action: Defer optimizer/captain extraction until exact scoring fixtures exist.

scores.evidence: scripts/lib/teamBuilderPublicModel.mjs and teamBuilderPublicHelpers.js share concepts and validators prove wrapper parity.
scores.remaining_risks: Browser helper wrapper is manually mirrored from the Node helper, which creates future drift risk.

scores.evidence: Local and deployed Playwright QA cover index and world-cup pages across five viewports, exact Team Builder assertions, profile clicks, old globals, console errors, and blocking failed requests.
scores.remaining_risks: Browser QA depends on Playwright availability and environment variables for the bundled runtime in sandboxed sessions.
scores.recommended_action: Keep using the active QA runner plus deployed reruns after public JS changes.

scores.evidence: Public payload contract passes and slimmed wrapper sizes are tracked in the manifest; current main wrappers total about 6.56 MB including recommendations, projections, score predictions, Team Builder artifact, helper, and script.
scores.remaining_risks: Projection and recommendation wrappers remain large for a static site.
scores.recommended_action: Defer further slimming unless page load metrics become a client concern.

scores.evidence: Homepage and world-cup page include Final Round framing, manual lock/lineup caveats, and final-squad source-backing caveats.
scores.remaining_risks: Users can still overinterpret projections as certainty.
scores.recommended_action: Keep current caveats; avoid new claims.

scores.evidence: Private files remain untracked; active manifest search checks block refereeing/conspiracy terms in public pages.
scores.remaining_risks: Untracked private files remain present locally and must not be staged.
scores.recommended_action: Leave private artifacts untracked until there is an explicit publication decision.

scores.evidence: The site uses static browser-ready wrappers and no runtime fetch for players or rules, matching AGENTS.md.
scores.remaining_risks: Static wrappers duplicate readable JSON and require careful sync discipline.
scores.recommended_action: Keep static architecture for current use; automate wrapper generation only in a separate safe pass.

scores.evidence: Final Round default, fixture context, Team Builder, caveats, and browser QA indicate the experience is presentable for a client-facing fantasy planning demo.
scores.remaining_risks: The tool is still planning help, not official advice.
scores.recommended_action: Present as an independent planning aid with caveats.

top_findings: The public site is safe to show with existing caveats.
The Team Builder is safe enough for public use because the default path remains generated-artifact-backed and exact browser assertions pass.
The active Final Round data path is protected by manifest wiring, validators, browser QA, and leakage searches.
script.js remains large, but the remaining extraction targets are not tiny and should be deferred.
The helper layer is useful, but generated browser wrapper parity should be a later maintainability improvement.
